metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify safe CLI invocation patterns for the private-key provisioning steps: use `aws ssm put-parameter --value file://path/to/keyfile` (or pipe via `--value "$(< keyfile)"` with `set +o history` bracketing) and `gh secret set REBAR_BOT_SIGNING_KEY < path/to/keyfile` to ensure the raw key never appears as a CLI argument in shell history or process listings.
findings.finding: The plan's operator steps for provisioning the private signing key to SSM and GitHub do not specify safe CLI invocation patterns, leaving open the risk that the raw ed25519 private key is passed as a CLI argument and leaks into shell history, process listings, or terminal scrollback.
findings.location: Plan body — 'Provision the PRIVATE key VALUE into both secret stores' operator steps; AC bullet 4 (`aws ssm put-parameter --type SecureString`; `gh secret set`)
findings.suggested_fix:
In the 'Provision the PRIVATE key VALUE' section, replace the bare command stubs with:
```bash
# Suppress history for this block
set +o history
aws ssm put-parameter \
  --name /rebar/prod/rebar-bot-signing-key \
  --type SecureString \
  --overwrite \
  --value file:///path/outside/repo/rebar-bot-ed25519
gh secret set REBAR_BOT_SIGNING_KEY \
  --repo navapbc/rebar \
  < /path/outside/repo/rebar-bot-ed25519
set -o history
# Shred the key file
shred -u /path/outside/repo/rebar-bot-ed25519
```
This keeps the raw key value out of CLI arguments (and therefore out of shell history, /proc/cmdline, and ps output) and replaces the vague 'discarded after' with an explicit `shred -u` step.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Strengthen AC4's SSM proving command to cross-verify the provisioned key matches the registered public key — e.g., derive the public key from the SSM value and compare its fingerprint against the identity ticket's registered key: `ssh-keygen -y -f <(aws ssm get-parameter --name /rebar/prod/rebar-bot-signing-key --with-decryption --query Parameter.Value --output text) | ssh-keygen -lf -` and assert the fingerprint matches the one used in `rebar identity create`.
findings.finding: AC4's SSM proving command (`aws ssm get-parameter --with-decryption`) only verifies the parameter is readable, not that it contains the CORRECT private key. An operator could provision the wrong key and this AC would pass silently.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4: 'aws ssm get-parameter --name /rebar/prod/rebar-bot-signing-key --with-decryption succeeds'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a cross-check step: `ssh-keygen -y -f <(aws ssm get-parameter --name /rebar/prod/rebar-bot-signing-key --with-decryption --query Parameter.Value --output text)` should produce a public key whose fingerprint matches the public key stored in the Rebar Bot identity ticket. This can be appended to AC4 as a second clause.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify idempotency behavior for `rebar identity create`: either document that the command is a no-op if an identity with the same email already exists, add a pre-flight `rebar list --type identity` check to the runbook, or note that the operator must manually delete a duplicate before re-running.
findings.finding: The idempotency of `rebar identity create` is unspecified — if the bootstrap command is run a second time (retry after partial failure, or accidental re-run), the plan gives no guidance on whether a duplicate identity ticket is created, or how to detect and clean up a duplicate.
findings.location: Plan body — `rebar identity create --name "Rebar Bot" …` step
findings.suggested_fix: Add a pre-flight guard to the runbook: `rebar list --type identity | python3 -c "import sys,json; ids=[i for i in json.load(sys.stdin) if i.get('email')=='joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com']; assert len(ids)==0, f'Identity already exists: {ids}'"` before running `rebar identity create`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace or supplement AC1's `rebar list --type identity` proof with a command that uses a documented, stable interface — e.g. `rebar show <identity_ticket_id>` (once the ID is known from `rebar identity create` stdout) or `rebar search` — OR explicitly note that `--type identity` is a supported extension of the `--type` filter.
findings.finding: `rebar list --type identity` (the AC1 proving command) is an undocumented `--type` value: the `list` help text and CLI reference enumerate only `bug, epic, story, task` as valid `--type` filter values. Although the code mechanically supports it (identity is in `_NON_GRAPH_ARTIFACT_TYPES`, so `exclude_session_logs` is set False and the type filter passes through), there is no validation rejection and no documented guarantee that this invocation surface is stable.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC1: `rebar list --type identity` proving command
findings.suggested_fix: Change AC1 to: capture the ticket ID emitted by `rebar identity create` (printed to stdout as the last whitespace-separated token), then verify with `rebar show <id>` and assert `ticket_type == "identity"` and the public key is present. This uses only documented, validated CLI surfaces.

findings.finding: AC3's `gh secret list` check is stated as prose ('shows REBAR_BOT_SIGNING_KEY') with no anchored grep or exit-code assertion, making it a defect-type-1 substring false-positive risk and leaving the executor with no deterministic pass/fail command.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3: '`gh secret list --repo navapbc/rebar` shows `REBAR_BOT_SIGNING_KEY`'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the prose description with: `gh secret list --repo navapbc/rebar | grep -wF 'REBAR_BOT_SIGNING_KEY' && echo PASS`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact repository path and the setup commands for AC2's preconditions — name the store repo directory and include the `git config user.email joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com` command as an explicit setup step before running the resolver check.
findings.finding: AC2's proving command requires preconditions (a repo directory with `git config user.email = joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com` and no `.rebar/current_identity` file) but the plan does not specify WHICH repository directory the operator should run the command in, leaving the verification environment ambiguous.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2: 'with the store repo's git config user.email = joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC2: 'Run from the `/path/to/rebar-store` repo (or whichever repo is configured as the Rebar identity store). Setup: `git -C <store-repo-path> config user.email joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com` (test-only; revert afterward). Then run the python command and assert the returned id matches the Rebar Bot ticket id from AC1.'
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `--overwrite` to the documented `aws ssm put-parameter` command (or note it explicitly) so the provisioning step is idempotent and supports re-runs/key rotation.
findings.finding: The `aws ssm put-parameter` command is not specified with `--overwrite`, making the SSM write non-idempotent: a second run (e.g., after a partial failure or key rotation) will fail with ParameterAlreadyExists rather than updating the value.
findings.location: Plan body: 'AWS SSM SecureString `/rebar/prod/rebar-bot-signing-key` (`aws ssm put-parameter --type SecureString`)'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the documented command hint to: `aws ssm put-parameter --name /rebar/prod/rebar-bot-signing-key --type SecureString --value <private-key> --overwrite` and add a note that `--overwrite` is required for re-runs and key rotation.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `--overwrite` to the `aws ssm put-parameter` command in the provisioning step so it succeeds in the Terraform-first ordering (CHANGEME placeholder already exists), consistent with every other out-of-band SSM population in the repo.
findings.finding: The plan's `aws ssm put-parameter --type SecureString` invocation omits `--overwrite`, causing it to fail with `ParameterAlreadyExists` when the expected Terraform-first ordering is followed: Terraform creates the CHANGEME placeholder via `apply`, then the operator tries to populate the real value — but `put-parameter` without `--overwrite` will reject an existing parameter name.
findings.location: Plan body — 'Provision the PRIVATE key VALUE into both secret stores' / bullet: 'AWS SSM SecureString /rebar/prod/rebar-bot-signing-key (`aws ssm put-parameter --type SecureString`'
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'aws ssm put-parameter --type SecureString' to 'aws ssm put-parameter --overwrite --type SecureString' (matching the pattern in infra/runbooks/gerrit-auth-hardening.md:39-41 and provision-restore.md:99).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `--overwrite` to the `aws ssm put-parameter` command in the plan's provisioning step so the SSM write is idempotent and safe to re-run during retries or key rotation.
findings.finding: The `aws ssm put-parameter` operator step in the plan body omits `--overwrite`, making the SSM provisioning step non-idempotent: a second run (e.g., after a partial failure, key rotation, or re-bootstrap) will fail with a `ParameterAlreadyExists` error rather than succeeding.
findings.location: Plan body — 'Provision the PRIVATE key VALUE into both secret stores' bullet, AWS SSM sub-bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Change the stated command hint to: `aws ssm put-parameter --name /rebar/prod/rebar-bot-signing-key --type SecureString --value "$(cat /path/to/private_key)" --overwrite`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a key-rotation runbook (or at minimum a plan note) covering: generate new keypair → `aws ssm put-parameter --overwrite` → `gh secret set` → `rebar identity update` or re-create → verify resolver — and specify that the old identity ticket should be retired/deleted.
findings.finding: No key rotation or identity-ticket revocation path is specified. The signing keypair is a long-lived shared credential; if the private key is compromised or a team member leaves, there is no stated procedure to rotate the SSM value, update the GitHub Actions secret, and revoke/replace the identity ticket atomically.
findings.location: Plan body — 'Provision the PRIVATE key VALUE into both secret stores' section; Acceptance Criteria (no rotation AC)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the expected failure mode of each consumer when the secret is absent or still CHANGEME (e.g., 'fetch-secrets.sh exits non-zero and fails the job with a clear error message'), and note that the GH Actions secret and SSM value should be populated before any workflow that uses them is triggered (sequencing constraint).
findings.finding: Consumer tolerance of a missing or transiently unavailable secret is not addressed. The plan notes that consumption wiring in 245e/9e76/1787 'already references these already-provisioned secrets,' but does not specify what those consumers do if the SSM fetch fails or if provisioning is only partially complete (e.g., SSM populated but GH Actions secret not yet set, or vice versa).
findings.location: Plan body — last bullet 'The per-env consumption wiring … references these already-provisioned secrets'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify that the `REBAR_BOT_SIGNING_KEY` secret is the raw ed25519 private key to be surfaced as `REBAR_IDENTITY_SIGNING_KEY` in each bot environment, and add an explicit mapping note (or rename the GH secret to `REBAR_IDENTITY_SIGNING_KEY` to match the established convention directly).
findings.finding: The plan names the GitHub Actions secret `REBAR_BOT_SIGNING_KEY`, but the codebase's established env var for injecting an SSH private key into the authorship-signing path is `REBAR_IDENTITY_SIGNING_KEY` (documented in `src/rebar/_config_schema.py:314` and `docs/env-vars.md`). The plan does not acknowledge this existing convention or specify the mapping from `REBAR_BOT_SIGNING_KEY` → `REBAR_IDENTITY_SIGNING_KEY` that downstream wiring (stories 9e76/1787) would need to provide. Without that bridge, a reader cannot verify the provisioned secret actually activates authorship signing on the bot.
findings.location: Plan body — 'GitHub Actions secret `REBAR_BOT_SIGNING_KEY`' provisioning step; AC4
findings.suggested_fix: Either rename the GH Actions secret to `REBAR_IDENTITY_SIGNING_KEY` (directly matching the established env var) or add a plan note: 'downstream wiring in 9e76/1787 must set `REBAR_IDENTITY_SIGNING_KEY=${{ secrets.REBAR_BOT_SIGNING_KEY }}` in the bot job env to activate authorship signing.'

findings.finding: The new SSM parameter `/rebar/prod/rebar-bot-signing-key` is provisioned entirely out-of-band via raw CLI (`aws ssm put-parameter`) and is NOT added to `rebar_secret_params` in `infra/terraform/ssm.tf`. Every other `/rebar/prod/*` secret in the codebase is declared as a Terraform-managed placeholder resource with `lifecycle { ignore_changes = [value] }`. Omitting this parameter from the IaC baseline means: (1) it is invisible to future `terraform plan` runs, (2) the existing Terraform drift-detection workflow will not flag if it is accidentally deleted or misconfigured, (3) there is no as-code teardown path, and (4) the established 'Terraform owns existence + type; operator owns value' contract (documented in ssm.tf header and ADR-0008) is silently broken for this one parameter.
findings.location: Plan body — AWS SSM provisioning step; `infra/terraform/ssm.tf` — `rebar_secret_params` list
findings.suggested_fix: Add `"/rebar/prod/rebar-bot-signing-key",` to the `rebar_secret_params` list in `infra/terraform/ssm.tf` with a comment referencing this ticket. The `lifecycle { ignore_changes = [value] }` block already on the `aws_ssm_parameter.rebar_secrets` resource ensures Terraform won't overwrite the operator-populated real value on subsequent applies.

findings.finding: `infra/terraform/ssm.tf` is not enumerated as a file to update, yet it manages ALL `/rebar/prod/*` SecureString parameters via `for_each = toset(local.rebar_secret_params)` and carries an explicit 'keep in sync' comment. The plan's imperative `aws ssm put-parameter` creates the `/rebar/prod/rebar-bot-signing-key` parameter entirely out-of-band from Terraform, leaving it untracked by IaC.
findings.location: Plan body — provisioning step: `aws ssm put-parameter --type SecureString`
findings.suggested_fix: In `infra/terraform/ssm.tf`, add `"/rebar/prod/rebar-bot-signing-key"` to the `rebar_secret_params` list (e.g., after `reviewbot-tickets-pat`). The operator's `aws ssm put-parameter --overwrite` populates the real value post-apply, exactly as with other secrets in this file.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
